body.title: Reply emission as pursuit-child — parent resumes for residue scan
body.result: residue_scan.py stub module + main.py hook after resume_parent. reply_state now threads input_text + reply_text + addressed_span. reply pursuit carries input_text in metadata. Exception-wrapped so hook failures can't regress reply path. 5 tests green. Commit a7dfe1b8.
body.description:
Currently, reply-sent = turn-done. The fast reflex path can't emit 'Hi!' and then keep processing the rest of Akien's message for follow-up content (the monkey-brain 'Hi! ... oh, and about X...' pattern). The pursuit layer already models parent-child suspend/resume (shipped in D-pursuit-layer + T-reply-forms-pursuit). Build on that: reflex-reply becomes a child pursuit that completes fast, parent pursuit resumes and triggers the residue scan (T-salience-residue-scan). Reply emission must be non-terminal — the pipeline keeps processing after reply-send.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/main.py` reply-emission path (HIGH-inertia — PRE-APPROVED by Akien 2026-04-22 as part of D-preparse-architecture blanket approval); likely `wild_igor/igor/pursuits.py` or the pursuit spawn helper; `tests/test_non_terminal_emission.py` new.
**Design rules:** docs-in-code (document the non-terminal pattern in main.py + pursuits.py); test-plan-or-why-not (covered); oop-first (extend pursuit class, don't re-implement).
**Scope boundary:** IN: reflex-reply → child pursuit that completes on emission, parent resumes with input-minus-addressed-portion for further processing. OUT: the residue-scan logic itself (T-salience-residue-scan); continuation-reply coherence markers (T-continuation-reply-register — TBD if needed).
**Test plan:** reflex-reply fires, child pursuit completes, parent pursuit resumes without blocking; input state carried across resume includes what-was-addressed flag; if parent's residue-scan returns nothing-interesting, no second reply fires; if something interesting, a new child pursuit for continuation reply spawns correctly; pipeline doesn't terminate on first emit.
